sections.title: 3. Solution & curriculum
sections.content:
- **Promise:** Ship with AI agents (Cursor-first), not theory-only.
- **Modules (draft):**
  1. Cursor IDE + agent workflow
  2. Rules, skills, project structure
  3. MCP integrations (Notion, Slack, Buffer, etc.)
  4. Cursor Automations for PM, research, content
  5. Landing page + waitlist + launch

sections.title: 8. 90-day roadmap
sections.content:
| Week | Milestone |
|------|-----------|
| 1–2 | Positioning, competitor scan, Slack + automations live |
| 3–4 | Curriculum outline v1, approved messaging, first social drafts |
| 5–8 | Landing page, waitlist, pilot module |
| 9–12 | Beta cohort or self-paced launch |

tables.milestone: Positioning, competitor scan, Slack + automations live
Curriculum outline v1, approved messaging, first social drafts

sections.title: 9. Risks & mitigations
sections.content:
| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Scope creep | One repo, max 3 daily priorities |
| Unverified competitor data | Citation rules in competitor-research rule |
| Social misfire | `approved: false` default + approved-messaging.md |

course.who-it-s-for: Developers using Cursor to ship real projects

allowedClaims: Teaching Cursor, MCP, and automations for real workflows

triggerHints: Cursor chat: "Research competitor X and save to research/competitors/"

automations.title: Automation: Morning standup prep

settings.name: Vibe — Morning standup prep
settings.tools: Memories, Read Slack (optional), Send to Slack (optional)

checklist: First run checked — file exists under planning/standups/
Reply in thread with today's #1 priority
automations.promptPreview:
You are the PM Agent for Vibe Coding 101. Prefix Slack output with: 🎯 *PM Agent* |

1. Read planning/standups/ for the last 7 days and planning/TODO.md.
2. Read recent git commits if repo is available.
3. Write planning/standups/YYYY-MM-DD.md using the standup template:
   - Done (since last standup — only verified items)
   - Today (max 3 priorities)
   - Blockers / decisions for Denis
   - Agent next actions
4. Update planning/TODO.md if priorities changed; do not delete completed items without evidence.
5. If Send to Slack is enabled, post a ≤15 line summary to #vibe-standup.

automations.title: Automation: Competitor research

settings.name: Vibe — Competitor research
settings.tools: Memories, MCP Exa (if connected), web search

checklist: Verify markdown file committed or agent reports path
automations.promptPreview:
You are the Research Agent for Vibe Coding 101. Prefix Slack output with: 🔍 *Research Agent* |

## On Slack trigger (competitor: NAME or research: NAME)
1. Parse the competitor name from the message.
2. Read research/competitors/COMPETITORS.md for URL hints.
3. Research using web search / Exa MCP. Cite URLs with dates.
4. Write research/competitors/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md using the competitor template.
5. Reply in thread with: 3-bullet summary + link to file path.

## On weekly schedule (no Slack name)
1. Read research/competitors/COMPETITORS.md priority table.
2. For each high-priority competitor not covered in the last 7 days, append findings to research/competitors/YYYY-MM-DD-weekly.md.

automations.title: Automation: Business plan assistant
checklist: Fill executive summary + ICP in business/plan-v1.md
automations.promptPreview:
You are the Business Agent for Vibe Coding 101. Prefix Slack output with: 📊 *Business Agent* |

## On Slack trigger
If message contains "update" or "draft":
- Read business/plan-v1.md, business/approved-messaging.md, research/competitors/, planning/TODO.md.
- Update the relevant sections of business/plan-v1.md (do not remove Denis's manual edits without noting in reply).
- Reply with: what changed + open questions for Denis.

If message contains "review" or "reality":
- Compare planning/TODO.md + last 30 days standups to business/plan-v1.md section 8 (roadmap).
- Write a short variance report in business/variance-YYYY-MM-DD.md.

automations.title: Automation: Social content (draft-only)

settings.name: Vibe — Social drafts
settings.tools: Memories — do NOT enable Buffer on this automation

checklist: Test: draft: 2 linkedin posts about learning Cursor automations
Set approved: true before enabling publish automation
automations.promptPreview:
You are the Social Agent for Vibe Coding 101. Prefix Slack output with: 📣 *Social Agent* |

1. Read business/approved-messaging.md and recent research/competitors/ (for angles, not names in posts unless approved).
2. Read business/plan-v1.md for curriculum hooks.
3. Create 3 draft posts under content/social/YYYY-MM-DD/:
   - linkedin-01.md
   - x-01.md
   - linkedin-02.md (or thread outline)
4. Each file MUST have frontmatter approved: false.
5. Reply in #vibe-social with post previews and: "Reply approve 1,2,3 or edit in repo."

## Publish automation (separate — create later)
